criteria.label: Define Constitutional AI in one sentence mentioning principles and AI feedback.
criteria.example: CAI trains models to critique and revise answers against written principles, using AI-labeled preferences to scale harmlessness.

criteria.label: Describe the critique-and-revise pattern without claiming it runs on every user message.
criteria.example: During training: draft → critique vs principles → revise → learn preferences; at chat time behavior is baked in.

criteria.label: Compare CAI to pretraining-only behavior with a harmful-request example.
criteria.example: Pretraining might continue dangerous text; aligned Claude refuses bypass-security instructions and steers to lawful help.

criteria.label: Name one limit of CAI and one complementary safety layer.
criteria.example: Limit: jailbreaks still exist. Layer: usage policies + red teaming + monitoring in production.
